relationship.description: Each student has exactly one set of contact details
One event type classifies many events
A student can register for multiple events
An event can have many registrations
Each registration is assigned one status (e.g., pending, confirmed, cancelled)
Each registration yields at most one result record
A result may be linked to an award (e.g., medal) or not

metadata.improvements_made: Added a REGISTRATION_STATUS lookup entity and changed REGISTRATION.status to a foreign key (status_code) to enforce ENUM values. Declared a UNIQUE constraint on (student_id, event_id) in REGISTRATION to prevent duplicate registrations (not shown in JSON but part of the physical design). Introduced a CHECK constraint concept on RESULT.performance to validate units according to the associated EVENT_TYPE. Suggested indexes on all foreign‑key columns (student_id, event_id, event_type_id, registration_id, result_id, status_code) for performance. Documented business rules (e.g., results only entered after event_date) to be enforced via triggers or application logic. Retained the separate CONTACT_INFO entity for flexibility while noting it could be merged into STUDENT for very small deployments.
